sourceMovie.title: We Can Be Heroes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto We Can Be Heroes (2020) as a family theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The family and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If We Can Be Heroes clicked for you, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after We Can Be Heroes?
faq.answer: Try Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, The Boxtrolls, Slumberland—each shares DNA with We Can Be Heroes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is We Can Be Heroes?
faq.answer: We Can Be Heroes (2020) is best described as Family, Action, Fantasy, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is We Can Be Heroes part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Sharkboy and Lavagirl Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
